1.name: Easy
1.description: Factual questions directly from the text

2.name: Medium
2.description: Interpretive questions about meaning and themes

questions.question: What did God create in the beginning?

options.A: The heaven and the earth

questions.explanation: Genesis 1:1 states 'In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.'

questions.question: What did God say 'Let there be' on the first day?

options.A: Light

questions.explanation: Genesis 1:3 says 'And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.'

questions.question: What did God call the light and the darkness?

options.A: Day and Night

questions.explanation: Genesis 1:5 says 'God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night.'

questions.question: What did God call the dry land?

options.A: Earth

questions.explanation: Genesis 1:10 states 'And God called the dry land Earth.'

questions.question: What two great lights did God make?

options.A: The greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night
options.D: The morning star and evening star

questions.explanation: Genesis 1:16 says 'God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night.'

questions.question: In whose image did God say 'Let us make man'?

options.A: In our image, after our likeness
options.C: In the image of the animals

questions.explanation: Genesis 1:26 says 'Let us make man in our image, after our likeness.'

questions.question: How did God create man?

options.A: Male and female created He them

questions.explanation: Genesis 1:27 says 'male and female created he them.'

questions.question: On which day did God rest from His work?

options.A: The seventh day

questions.explanation: Genesis 2:2 says 'on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested.'

questions.question: What did God breathe into man's nostrils?

options.A: The breath of life

questions.explanation: Genesis 2:7 says 'and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.'

questions.question: Where did the LORD God plant a garden?

options.A: Eastward in Eden

questions.explanation: Genesis 2:8 says 'the LORD God planted a garden eastward in Eden.'

questions.question: What two special trees were in the garden?

options.A: The tree of life and the tree of knowledge of good and evil
options.B: The tree of wisdom and the tree of understanding
options.C: The tree of peace and the tree of joy
options.D: The tree of faith and the tree of hope

questions.explanation: Genesis 2:9 mentions 'the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.'

questions.question: What was man to do in the garden?

options.A: Dress it and keep it

questions.explanation: Genesis 2:15 says 'to dress it and to keep it.'

questions.question: What did God say would happen if Adam ate from the tree of knowledge?

options.A: Thou shalt surely die

questions.explanation: Genesis 2:17 says 'for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.'

questions.question: What did God say was not good?

options.A: That the man should be alone

questions.explanation: Genesis 2:18 says 'It is not good that the man should be alone.'

questions.question: What did God make from Adam's rib?

options.A: A woman

questions.explanation: Genesis 2:22 says 'made he a woman, and brought her unto the man.'

questions.question: Which animal was more subtle than any other?

options.A: The serpent

questions.explanation: Genesis 3:1 says 'the serpent was more subtle than any beast of the field.'

questions.question: What did Eve see about the tree?

options.A: That it was good for food and pleasant to the eyes
options.B: That it was tall and strong
options.C: That it was the only tree in the garden

questions.explanation: Genesis 3:6 says 'the tree was good for food... pleasant to the eyes.'

questions.question: What did Adam and Eve realize after eating the fruit?

options.A: That they were naked

questions.explanation: Genesis 3:7 says 'the eyes of them both were opened, and they knew that they were naked.'

questions.question: Where did Adam and Eve hide from the LORD God?

options.A: Among the trees of the garden

questions.explanation: Genesis 3:8 says 'hid themselves from the presence of the LORD God amongst the trees of the garden.'

questions.question: Who did Eve say beguiled her?

options.A: The serpent

questions.explanation: Genesis 3:13 says 'The serpent beguiled me, and I did eat.'

questions.question: What did God say man would return to?

options.A: Dust

questions.explanation: Genesis 3:19 says 'for dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return.'

questions.question: What was the name of Adam and Eve's first son?

options.A: Cain

questions.explanation: Genesis 4:1 says 'she conceived, and bare Cain.'

questions.question: What was Abel's occupation?

options.A: A keeper of sheep

questions.explanation: Genesis 4:2 says 'Abel was a keeper of sheep.'

questions.question: What did Cain do to Abel?

options.A: Slew him

questions.explanation: Genesis 4:8 says 'Cain rose up against Abel his brother, and slew him.'

questions.question: How many years did Adam live?

options.A: 930 years

questions.explanation: Genesis 5:5 says 'all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years.'

questions.question: What happened to Enoch?

options.A: God took him

questions.explanation: Genesis 5:24 says 'Enoch walked with God: and he was not; for God took him.'

questions.question: Who found grace in the eyes of the LORD?

options.A: Noah

questions.explanation: Genesis 6:8 says 'But Noah found grace in the eyes of the LORD.'

questions.question: What was Noah instructed to build?

options.A: An ark

questions.explanation: Genesis 6:14 says 'Make thee an ark of gopher wood.'

questions.question: When did the flood begin?

options.A: In the six hundredth year of Noah's life

questions.explanation: Genesis 7:11 says 'In the six hundredth year of Noah's life... the same day were all the fountains... broken up.'

questions.question: Where did the ark rest?

options.A: Upon the mountains of Ararat

questions.explanation: Genesis 8:4 says 'the ark rested... upon the mountains of Ararat.'

questions.question: What did the dove bring back to Noah?

options.A: An olive leaf

questions.explanation: Genesis 8:11 says 'in her mouth was an olive leaf pluckt off.'

questions.question: What did God set in the cloud as a sign of His covenant?

options.A: A bow (rainbow)

questions.explanation: Genesis 9:13 says 'I do set my bow in the cloud.'

questions.question: What was the language of the whole earth at first?

options.A: One language

questions.explanation: Genesis 11:1 says 'the whole earth was of one language, and of one speech.'

questions.question: What was the name of the tower where languages were confused?

options.A: Babel

questions.explanation: Genesis 11:9 says 'Therefore is the name of it called Babel.'

questions.question: What did the LORD tell Abram to leave?

options.A: His country, kindred, and father's house

questions.explanation: Genesis 12:1 says 'Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and from thy father's house.'

questions.question: How old was Abram when he departed from Haran?

options.A: Seventy and five years old

questions.explanation: Genesis 12:4 says 'Abram was seventy and five years old when he departed out of Haran.'

questions.question: Why could Abram and Lot not dwell together?

options.A: The land could not bear them because their substance was great

questions.explanation: Genesis 13:6 says 'the land was not able to bear them, that they might dwell together: for their substance was great.'

questions.question: Who brought forth bread and wine to Abram?

options.A: Melchizedek king of Salem

questions.explanation: Genesis 14:18 says 'Melchizedek king of Salem brought forth bread and wine.'

questions.question: What was counted to Abram for righteousness?

options.A: He believed in the LORD

questions.explanation: Genesis 15:6 says 'he believed in the LORD; and he counted it to him for righteousness.'

questions.question: What name was Hagar to call her son?

options.A: Ishmael

questions.explanation: Genesis 16:11 says 'shalt call his name Ishmael.'

questions.question: How old was Abram when the LORD appeared to him as the Almighty God?

options.A: Ninety years old and nine

questions.explanation: Genesis 17:1 says 'when Abram was ninety years old and nine.'

questions.question: What did God change Abram's name to?

options.A: Abraham

questions.explanation: Genesis 17:5 says 'thy name shall be Abraham.'

questions.question: How many men appeared to Abraham at Mamre?

options.A: Three

questions.explanation: Genesis 18:2 says 'three men stood by him.'

questions.question: What did the LORD rain upon Sodom and Gomorrah?

options.A: Brimstone and fire

questions.explanation: Genesis 19:24 says 'the LORD rained upon Sodom and upon Gomorrah brimstone and fire.'

questions.question: What name did Abraham give his son born through Sarah?

options.A: Isaac

questions.explanation: Genesis 21:3 says 'Abraham called the name of his son... Isaac.'

questions.question: Where was Abraham to offer Isaac as a burnt offering?

options.A: The land of Moriah

questions.explanation: Genesis 22:2 says 'get thee into the land of Moriah.'

questions.question: What was the name of the woman who came to the well and became Isaac's wife?

options.A: Rebekah

questions.explanation: Genesis 24:15 says 'Rebekah came out.'

questions.question: What did Esau look like when he was born?

options.A: Red, all over like a hairy garment

questions.explanation: Genesis 25:25 says 'the first came out red, all over like an hairy garment.'

questions.question: What did Jacob see in his dream at Bethel?

options.A: A ladder set up on the earth reaching to heaven with angels ascending and descending

questions.explanation: Genesis 28:12 says 'a ladder set up on the earth, and the top of it reached to heaven: and behold the angels of God ascending and descending on it.'

questions.question: What new name did God give to Jacob?

options.A: Israel

questions.explanation: Genesis 32:28 says 'Thy name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel.'

questions.question: What does the opening verse of Genesis teach us about the nature of God?

options.A: That God is the Creator of all things
options.B: That God is only a spirit
options.C: That God is distant from creation
options.D: That God did not create the world

questions.explanation: Genesis 1:1 reveals God as the sovereign Creator who existed before all things and brought the universe into existence.

questions.question: What is significant about God speaking light into existence?

options.A: It shows God's power to create by His word alone
options.B: It shows God needed materials to create
options.C: It shows God was limited in His power

questions.explanation: God's spoken word demonstrates His absolute authority and creative power without needing any pre-existing materials.

questions.question: What does 'Let us make man in our image' imply about God?

options.A: It implies the plurality and unity within the Godhead
options.B: It implies God was speaking to angels

questions.explanation: The plural 'us' and 'our' suggests a plurality of persons within the one God - consistent with the Trinity.

questions.question: Why did God rest on the seventh day?

options.A: To set an example of rest and sanctify the Sabbath
options.B: Because He was tired from creating
options.C: To show He was finished with creation permanently
options.D: Because He needed to rest like humans

questions.explanation: God's rest was not due to fatigue but to establish a pattern of rest and bless the Sabbath day for His creation.

questions.question: What motivated Eve to eat the forbidden fruit?

options.A: It was good for food, pleasant to the eyes, and desirable to make one wise
options.B: She was hungry and had no other food
options.D: She did not know it was forbidden

questions.explanation: Eve was tempted by physical desire, visual appeal, and the pride of becoming wise - three classic forms of temptation.

questions.question: What is the significance of the promise that the woman's seed would bruise the serpent's head?

options.A: It is the first prophecy of the coming Savior (Messiah)
options.B: It refers to all mankind defeating evil
options.C: It was fulfilled immediately by Adam

questions.explanation: This is known as the 'Protoevangelium' - the first gospel promise that a future descendant of Eve would ultimately defeat Satan.

questions.question: What does Cain's question 'Am I my brother's keeper?' reveal about his character?

options.A: It reveals his selfishness, defensiveness, and lack of responsibility for others
options.B: It shows he was genuinely confused
options.D: It shows he cared deeply for Abel

questions.explanation: Cain's response shows a hardened heart, refusing to acknowledge responsibility for his brother's welfare after murdering him.

questions.question: What does 'every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually' tell us about the condition of humanity before the flood?

options.A: Humanity had become completely corrupt and wicked
options.D: Only their thoughts were evil, not their actions

questions.explanation: This describes total depravity - that sin had corrupted every aspect of human nature before the flood.

questions.question: What does God's promise never to destroy the earth again with a flood teach us about His character?

options.A: God is merciful and keeps His promises despite human sin
options.B: God was unable to flood the earth again
options.C: God changed His mind about judging sin
options.D: God no longer cares about sin

questions.explanation: Despite knowing that 'the imagination of man's heart is evil from his youth,' God shows mercy by promising never to destroy all life with a flood again.

questions.question: Why does God require the death penalty for murder according to this verse?

options.A: Because man was made in the image of God
options.C: Because it was a cultural practice

questions.explanation: The sanctity of human life is based on the fact that humans are created in God's image, making murder an attack on God Himself.

questions.question: What was the underlying sin of those building the Tower of Babel?

options.A: Pride and the desire to make a name for themselves instead of glorifying God
options.D: They wanted to reach heaven physically

questions.explanation: Their goal was to 'make us a name' - seeking their own glory rather than obeying God's command to fill the earth.

questions.question: What was the purpose of God's blessing on Abram?

options.A: To make him a blessing to all families of the earth
options.D: To separate him from everyone else

questions.explanation: God blessed Abram not just for his own sake but so that 'all families of the earth be blessed' through him.

questions.question: What does Abram's faith being 'counted... for righteousness' teach about salvation?

options.A: Righteousness comes through faith, not works

questions.explanation: This verse is foundational for understanding justification by faith - Abram was declared righteous because he believed God, not because of his works.

questions.question: What does 'Is any thing too hard for the LORD?' teach us about God's power?

options.A: Nothing is impossible for God
options.B: God can do some things but not everything
options.C: God's power is limited by natural laws

questions.explanation: This rhetorical question affirms God's omnipotence - that He has unlimited power and nothing is impossible for Him.

questions.question: What was God testing when He asked Abraham to sacrifice Isaac?

options.A: Abraham's fear of God and willingness to obey

questions.explanation: God tested Abraham's devotion and obedience - showing that he feared God more than he loved even his promised son.

questions.question: What does Esau's willingness to sell his birthright for a meal reveal about his values?

options.A: He despised spiritual blessings for immediate physical gratification
options.B: He was very hungry and made a reasonable choice
options.C: He planned to get his birthright back later
options.D: He did not understand what he was doing

questions.explanation: Esau's choice showed he valued temporary, physical satisfaction more than the lasting spiritual significance of his birthright.

questions.question: Why was Jacob afraid after his dream at Bethel?

options.A: He realized he had encountered the presence of God
options.B: He was afraid of the ladder
options.C: He thought the dream was a bad omen
options.D: He was afraid of the angels

questions.explanation: Jacob was filled with awe and fear because he recognized that he had unexpectedly come into the presence of the holy God.

questions.question: What does Jacob's wrestling with God represent spiritually?

options.A: Perseverance in prayer and dependence on God

questions.explanation: Jacob's wrestling symbolizes persistent prayer and the struggle of faith - refusing to let go until God blesses.

questions.question: Why did Joseph's brothers hate him?

options.A: Their father loved Joseph more and gave him a special coat

questions.explanation: Jacob's favoritism toward Joseph, symbolized by the coat of many colors, fueled jealousy and hatred among the brothers.

questions.question: How did Joseph view his brothers selling him into slavery?

options.A: As God's plan to preserve life, not their evil intent
options.D: As something he would never forgive

questions.explanation: Joseph recognized God's sovereignty - the brothers meant evil, but God used it for good to save many lives.

questions.question: How does Joseph's statement 'ye thought evil against me; but God meant it unto good' teach about suffering?

options.A: God can redeem evil circumstances for good purposes

questions.explanation: Joseph models faith that God sovereignly works through human evil to accomplish His good purposes.

questions.question: Why did God expel Adam and Eve from the garden?

options.A: To prevent them from eating the tree of life and living forever in sin

questions.explanation: God showed mercy by preventing eternal life in a fallen, sinful condition.

questions.question: Why did God accept Abel's offering but not Cain's?

options.A: The heart attitude and faith behind the offering mattered more than the offering itself

questions.explanation: Hebrews 11:4 explains Abel offered by faith; the issue was not the offering but the heart.

questions.question: What are the three promises God made to Abram?

options.A: Land, descendants, and blessing to all nations

questions.explanation: God promised Abram land (Canaan), countless descendants, and that through him all nations would be blessed.

questions.question: Why did God tell Abram 'Fear not'?

options.A: I am thy shield and thy exceeding great reward

questions.explanation: God reassured Abram of His protection (shield) and provision (reward).

questions.question: What was the purpose of circumcision as a sign of the covenant?

options.A: An outward physical sign of the spiritual covenant between God and Abraham's descendants

questions.explanation: Circumcision was a visible sign marking those who belonged to God's covenant people.

questions.question: What does Abraham's intercession for Sodom demonstrate about God's justice?

options.A: God is willing to spare the righteous even among the wicked
options.B: God does not listen to prayer
options.C: God's mind can be changed by bargaining

questions.explanation: Abraham's dialogue shows God's justice separates judgment on the wicked from the righteous.

questions.question: Why did Lot's wife become a pillar of salt?

options.A: She looked back disobediently, showing attachment to Sodom
options.D: She was turned into salt as a punishment for her sins

questions.explanation: Her backward glance revealed her heart was still attached to the wicked city she was commanded to leave.

questions.question: What did Abraham mean when he said 'God will provide himself a lamb'?

options.A: A prophetic statement pointing to God's ultimate provision of Jesus as the Lamb
options.C: He hoped a lamb would appear

questions.explanation: Abraham prophetically spoke of God's provision, ultimately fulfilled in Christ, the Lamb of God.

questions.question: What comfort did Rebekah bring to Isaac?

options.A: He was comforted after his mother's death
options.D: She brought him a new home

questions.explanation: Rebekah's love comforted Isaac after the loss of his mother Sarah.

questions.question: Why did Esau say Jacob was rightly named?

options.A: Because Jacob (supplanter) had taken his birthright and blessing

questions.explanation: Esau connected Jacob's name (meaning 'supplanter') with his actions of taking both birthright and blessing.

questions.question: Why did seven years seem like a few days to Jacob?

options.A: Because of his great love for Rachel

questions.explanation: Jacob's deep love for Rachel made the long wait feel short.

questions.question: What did the name Mizpah ('The LORD watch between me and thee') signify?

options.A: A witness that God would oversee their covenant when apart

questions.explanation: Mizpah invoked God as the witness and guardian of their agreement when they were separated.

questions.question: Why did God change Jacob's name to Israel?

options.A: Because he had power with God and men and prevailed

questions.explanation: The name Israel ('prince with God') commemorated Jacob's wrestling with God and his new identity.

questions.question: For how much silver was Joseph sold by his brothers?

options.A: Twenty pieces of silver

questions.explanation: Genesis 37:28 says 'sold Joseph to the Ishmeelites for twenty pieces of silver.'

questions.question: Why did everything prosper in Joseph's hands?

options.A: The LORD was with Joseph

questions.explanation: Joseph's success was because 'the LORD was with Joseph' and blessed everything he did.

questions.question: To whom did Joseph give credit for interpreting dreams?

options.A: God

questions.explanation: Joseph humbly said 'It is not in me: God shall give Pharaoh an answer of peace.'

questions.question: What did Judah offer to do for Benjamin?

options.A: Become a slave in his place

questions.explanation: Judah offered himself as a substitute slave to spare Benjamin, showing character transformation.

questions.question: How did Jacob describe his years of life to Pharaoh?

options.A: Few and evil

questions.explanation: Jacob humbly said his 130 years were 'few and evil' compared to his fathers.

questions.question: What was special about Joseph's blessing from Jacob?

options.A: He received a greater portion and rich blessings from heaven and earth

questions.explanation: Jacob gave Joseph extensive blessings, including a double portion through his sons Ephraim and Manasseh.

questions.question: How does the description of the earth as 'without form, and void' contribute to the theological message of creation?

options.A: It emphasizes God's creation of order, light, and life from chaos and emptiness
options.B: It suggests God created the world in an imperfect state
options.C: It indicates a battle between God and chaos

questions.explanation: The initial chaos and darkness highlight God's power to bring order, light, and goodness into existence by His Word.

questions.question: What foundational principle for marriage is established in Genesis 2:24?

options.A: Leaving parents, cleaving to spouse, becoming one flesh

questions.explanation: Jesus quotes this verse to establish God's original design for marriage as a permanent, exclusive union.

questions.question: What pattern of temptation is shown in Eve's encounter with the serpent?

options.A: Doubt God's word, denial of consequences, desire for what's forbidden

questions.explanation: The serpent first created doubt about God's command, then denied judgment, then appealed to pride.

questions.question: What does it mean that Noah 'walked with God'?

options.A: He lived in constant fellowship and obedience to God

questions.explanation: Walking with God describes a life of close relationship, trust, and obedience.

questions.question: What is the significance of Noah's curse on Canaan and blessing on Shem and Japheth?

options.A: It prophetically describes the future relationship of these peoples
options.B: It was just an angry father's words

questions.explanation: This prophecy foreshadows Israel's conquest of Canaan (descendants of Ham) and God's blessing through Shem.

questions.question: What does Abram's deception in Egypt reveal about his faith at this point?

options.A: His faith was still developing; he feared man more than trusting God
options.C: He was a liar by nature
options.D: He did not believe in God

questions.explanation: Abram's fear of Pharaoh shows his faith was not yet fully mature, despite earlier obedience.

questions.question: Why is Melchizedek significant in biblical theology?

options.A: He is a type of Christ as both king and priest

questions.explanation: Melchizedek, king of Salem and priest of God, prefigures Jesus who is a priest forever after his order (Hebrews 7).

questions.question: What was the spiritual problem with Abram and Sarai's plan to have a child through Hagar?

options.A: They took matters into their own hands instead of waiting for God's timing
options.C: They should have used a different servant

questions.explanation: Their lack of faith led them to use cultural practices instead of trusting God's promise in His timing.

questions.question: What does the sin of Sodom reveal about the extent of their wickedness?

options.A: Their sexual immorality and violent rejection of hospitality showed total depravity

questions.explanation: The men of Sodom demanded to sexually assault the visitors, showing profound wickedness deserving judgment.

questions.question: What is the theological significance of Abraham's near-sacrifice of Isaac?

options.A: It foreshadows God's sacrifice of His own Son
options.B: It proves Abraham was willing to kill
options.C: It shows God changed His mind
options.D: It was a test of physical strength

questions.explanation: This story prefigures God the Father offering His Son Jesus as the ultimate sacrifice.

questions.question: What does Jacob's deception to receive Isaac's blessing teach about God's sovereignty?

options.A: God's purposes prevail despite human sin and schemes

questions.explanation: Despite Jacob's deception, God's chosen line continued through Jacob as He had ordained before their birth (Romans 9).

questions.question: What does Laban's deception of Jacob teach about reaping what we sow?

options.A: Jacob, who deceived his father, was now deceived by his uncle

questions.explanation: There is poetic justice - Jacob the deceiver experienced deception himself, refining his character.

questions.question: Why did Jacob demand a blessing from the man he wrestled?

options.A: He recognized this was a divine encounter and refused to let go without assurance
options.C: He wanted to win the fight

questions.explanation: Jacob's persistence shows the principle of persevering prayer - clinging to God until He blesses.

questions.question: What was the problem with Simeon and Levi's revenge on Shechem?

options.A: They used deceit and excessive violence, bringing shame on Jacob's family
options.B: They were right to kill everyone
options.C: They should have killed more people

questions.explanation: Their disproportionate revenge broke the covenant they had made under false pretenses, violating trust.

questions.question: Why did Jacob rebuke Joseph for his dream but also 'observed the saying'?

options.A: He was offended but recognized it might be prophetic

questions.explanation: Jacob's response shows the tension between natural offense at the dream's implications and openness to God's revelation.

questions.question: Why was Onan's action considered wicked by the LORD?

options.A: He refused to raise up offspring for his brother, disobeying the duty of a levirate marriage

questions.explanation: Onan's refusal to fulfill his family duty was an act of disobedience and selfishness against God's commands.

questions.question: What does Joseph's interpretation of the cupbearer and baker's dreams teach about God's timing?

options.A: God's plans unfold in His perfect timing, not Joseph's

questions.explanation: Joseph had to wait two more years after successfully interpreting the dreams before his own deliverance came.

questions.question: Why did Pharaoh dream the same message twice?

options.A: To show the matter was firmly established by God and would happen soon

questions.explanation: Joseph explained that the double dream confirmed God's decree and its imminence.

questions.question: What did Joseph's brothers recognize as the cause of their current distress?

options.A: Their guilt over how they treated Joseph

questions.explanation: Conscience awakened; they connected their suffering to their sin against Joseph years earlier.

questions.question: Why did Joseph give Benjamin five times more food than the others?

options.A: To test his brothers' reaction for jealousy toward Benjamin as they had shown toward him

questions.explanation: Joseph was testing whether his brothers had changed - would they envy Benjamin as they had envied him?

questions.question: How does Judah's speech show character transformation?

options.A: He offered himself as a substitute slave for Benjamin, unlike his earlier selfishness

questions.explanation: The same Judah who suggested selling Joseph now offered himself to protect his brother, showing genuine change.

questions.question: What did Joseph mean by 'See that ye fall not out by the way'?

options.A: Don't argue or blame each other about the past on the journey home

questions.explanation: Joseph was warning against quarreling over who was responsible for selling him - he had forgiven them.

questions.question: Why did God tell Jacob not to fear going down to Egypt?

options.A: Because He would go with him and bring him back

questions.explanation: God's presence and promise of return addressed Jacob's fear of leaving the Promised Land.

questions.question: Why did Jacob cross his hands to bless Ephraim before Manasseh?

options.A: Guided by the Spirit to give the greater blessing to the younger
options.B: He was confused due to blindness

questions.explanation: Jacob intentionally blessed the younger son, continuing the pattern of God choosing the unexpected.

questions.question: Why did Reuben lose his birthright and blessing as firstborn?

options.A: He defiled his father's bed by sleeping with Bilhah

questions.explanation: Reuben's sin with Bilhah disqualified him from the privileges and responsibilities of the firstborn.

questions.question: What is significant about Judah's blessing compared to his brothers'?

options.A: It includes the promise of kingship and the coming Messiah (Shiloh)

questions.explanation: Judah received the ruling scepter and the promise of the Messiah, the line of David and Jesus.

questions.question: What does the Egyptian embalming of Jacob (Israel) signify about the family's integration into Egypt?

options.A: They had become influential and respected in Egyptian society
options.C: They were forced to adopt Egyptian customs
options.D: The Egyptians took over their burial

questions.explanation: The Egyptian-style embalming, typically reserved for royalty, shows Jacob's high status in Egypt.

questions.question: What does Joseph's response to his brothers' fear reveal about genuine forgiveness?

options.A: True forgiveness releases others' guilt and trusts God's sovereignty

questions.explanation: Joseph wept at their fear, assured them of his forgiveness, and reframed their evil in light of God's good plan.

questions.question: Why did Joseph make the Israelites swear to carry his bones to Canaan?

options.A: As an act of faith that God would bring them back to the Promised Land
options.C: Because his brothers asked him to

questions.explanation: Joseph's request demonstrated faith in God's promise to return Abraham's descendants to Canaan.

questions.question: What is the literary and theological significance of Genesis ending with Joseph in a coffin in Egypt?

options.A: It leaves the story unfinished, pointing to Exodus and God's future deliverance
options.B: It's a sad ending with no hope

questions.explanation: The open-ended conclusion sets up the need for Exodus - God's people are in Egypt awaiting redemption.

questions.question: What did Joseph's name mean according to Rachel?

options.A: The LORD shall add another son

questions.explanation: Rachel said 'The LORD shall add to me another son,' connecting Joseph's name to addition and hope.

questions.question: How did Esau greet Jacob when they finally met?

options.A: He ran to meet him, embraced him, kissed him, and they wept

questions.explanation: Esau's surprising embrace shows God had softened his heart, granting reconciliation.

questions.question: Why does Genesis include the detailed genealogy of Esau (Edom)?

options.A: To show God's faithfulness to Esau's descendants and Israel's connection to neighboring nations
options.C: To show Esau was more important

questions.explanation: The genealogy honors Esau as Abraham's son and explains Israel's relationship with Edom.

questions.question: What did Jacob and Laban name the heap of stones marking their covenant?

options.A: Galeed and Mizpah

questions.explanation: Jacob called it Galeed, Laban called it Jegarsahadutha, and Mizpah was the watchtower name.

questions.question: What did Laban do before parting from Jacob?

options.A: Kissed his sons and daughters and blessed them

questions.explanation: Laban departed after kissing his daughters and grandchildren and blessing them goodbye.

questions.question: What did Jacob build for his cattle at Succoth?

options.A: Booths

questions.explanation: Jacob made booths (succoth) for his cattle, giving the place its name.

questions.question: Who was Deborah in relation to Jacob's family?

options.A: Rebekah's nurse

questions.explanation: Deborah was Rebekah's nurse who died and was buried under an oak called Allonbachuth.

questions.question: What name did Rachel give her son before dying, and what did Jacob rename him?

options.A: Benoni, but Jacob called him Benjamin
options.B: Joseph, but Jacob called him Ephraim
options.C: Manasseh, but Jacob called him Naphtali
options.D: Levi, but Jacob called him Simeon

questions.explanation: Rachel named him Benoni ('son of my sorrow'), Jacob renamed him Benjamin ('son of the right hand').

questions.question: Where did Esau settle with his descendants?

options.A: Mount Seir

questions.explanation: Esau dwelt in mount Seir; Esau is Edom, and his descendants were the Edomites.

questions.question: What Egyptian name did Pharaoh give to Joseph?

options.A: Zaphnathpaaneah

questions.explanation: Pharaoh named Joseph Zaphnathpaaneah, meaning 'savior of the world' or 'revealer of secrets.'

questions.question: What were the names of Joseph's two sons and what did they mean?

options.A: Manasseh ('forget') and Ephraim ('fruitful')

questions.explanation: Manasseh means 'God has made me forget my toil' and Ephraim means 'God has made me fruitful.'

questions.question: What did Joseph's brothers do when they came before him in Egypt?

options.A: Bowed down to him with their faces to the earth

questions.explanation: They bowed before Joseph, unknowingly fulfilling his childhood dreams of their submission.

questions.question: How much more food was Benjamin given than the other brothers?

options.A: Five times as much

questions.explanation: Benjamin's portion was five times larger than the others, testing their jealousy.

questions.question: What did Joseph have placed in Benjamin's sack as a test?

options.A: His silver cup

questions.explanation: Joseph's silver cup was hidden in Benjamin's sack to create a test of the brothers' loyalty.

questions.question: Where did Joseph want his father and brothers to live in Egypt?

options.A: The land of Goshen

questions.explanation: Joseph specifically requested the land of Goshen for his family's settlement.

questions.question: How many people from Jacob's house came to Egypt?

options.A: Seventy

questions.explanation: Genesis 46:27 records that seventy persons from Jacob's family came to Egypt.

questions.question: How long did Jacob live in Egypt?

options.A: Seventeen years

questions.explanation: Jacob lived in Egypt for seventeen years, from age 130 to 147.

questions.question: Whom did Jacob adopt as his own sons, equal to Reuben and Simeon?

options.A: Ephraim and Manasseh

questions.explanation: Jacob adopted Joseph's two sons, giving them inheritance rights as his own.

questions.question: What did Jacob do after blessing his sons?

options.A: He died and was gathered to his people

questions.explanation: After commanding his sons, Jacob gathered up his feet into the bed and died.

questions.question: Where was Jacob buried?

options.A: In the cave of Machpelah near Mamre

questions.explanation: Jacob was buried in the cave of Machpelah, purchased by Abraham as a family burial place.

questions.question: What did God place at the east of the garden of Eden?

options.A: Cherubims and a flaming sword

questions.explanation: God placed cherubim and a flaming sword to guard the way to the tree of life.

questions.question: What name did Eve give her third son?

options.A: Seth

questions.explanation: Eve named her son Seth, meaning 'appointed,' as a replacement for Abel.

questions.question: At what age did Adam have a son in his own likeness?

options.A: 130 years

questions.explanation: Adam was 130 years old when he had Seth, who was in his own likeness.

questions.question: What were the dimensions of Noah's ark?

options.A: 300 cubits long, 50 cubits wide, 30 cubits high
options.B: 500 cubits long, 100 cubits wide, 50 cubits high
options.C: 200 cubits long, 30 cubits wide, 20 cubits high
options.D: 400 cubits long, 60 cubits wide, 40 cubits high

questions.explanation: The ark was huge: 300 cubits (450 feet) long, 50 cubits (75 feet) wide, 30 cubits (45 feet) high.

questions.question: What did God give as food for Noah after the flood?

options.A: Every moving thing that liveth (meat)

questions.explanation: God expanded human diet from plants only to include animal meat after the flood.

questions.question: Who was described as a mighty hunter before the LORD?

options.A: Nimrod

questions.explanation: Nimrod, son of Cush, was a mighty hunter whose kingdom began at Babel.

questions.question: How much silver did Abraham pay for the cave of Machpelah?

options.A: Four hundred shekels of silver

questions.explanation: Abraham weighed out four hundred shekels of silver for the field and cave.

questions.question: What gifts did Abraham's servant give to Rebekah at the well?

options.A: A golden earring and two bracelets

questions.explanation: The servant gave Rebekah a golden earring and two bracelets as betrothal gifts.

questions.question: What did Isaac do with the wells his father Abraham had dug?

options.A: He re-dug them and restored them

questions.explanation: Isaac re-dug his father's wells, which the Philistines had stopped up.

questions.question: What blessings did Isaac unknowingly give to Jacob?

options.A: Dew of heaven, fatness of earth, plenty of corn and wine, and rule over his brethren

questions.explanation: Isaac blessed Jacob with agricultural abundance, political rule, and the promise of blessing and cursing.
